title: How do the Eva pilots answer natures call
display_title: How do the Eva pilots answer natures call
wikitext:
[]: How do the Eva pilots answer nature's call?             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Sat Jul 29, 2006 8:53 am I mean, when you've been kicking an angel's butt for a while, you gotta take a whiz somewhile. If they peed in the LCL, they couldn't breathe the pee, so they'd drown and die from accidently breathing the urine.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Sat Jul 29, 2006 9:01 am I've thought about that before and asked a similar question. Do you remember when Shinji was trapped in the Sea of Dirac? The LCL eventually became cloudy. I've been told that was some metabolic wastes from the pilot. But anyway, the pilot isn't expected to stay in the Unit all day right?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Jul 29, 2006 12:22 pm Developing fetuses swallow amniotic fluid all the time. Along with developmental products, the amniotic fluid is also filled with plenty of waste products. The fetus is safe to take that stuff into its system. One could argue that since the LCL is like amniotic fluid, it is safe for the pilot to breathe it in (along with waste products like urine floating around in it). Of course, you could argue against this because a fetus' lungs are closed and an adult's (like an Eva pilot) are open.

[]: Re: How do the Eva pilots answer nature's call?             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 29, 2006 1:02 pm Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: I mean, when you've been kicking an angel's butt for a while, you gotta take a whiz somewhile. If they peed in the LCL, they couldn't breathe the pee, so they'd drown and die from accidently breathing the urine.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Strangelove » Sat Jul 29, 2006 1:05 pm Well, LCL is effectively, the same stuff you breethed for 9 months in your mom's womb. I'd imagine it really wouldn't make you need to..go..I supose. By that I mean, when you breath it in, it goes into your *lungs*, not your stomach.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 29, 2006 1:07 pm Dr. Strangelove wrote: Well, LCL is effectively, the same stuff you breethed for 9 months in your mom's womb. I'd imagine it really wouldn't make you need to..go..I supose. By that I mean, when you breath it in, it goes into your *lungs*, not your stomach.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 29, 2006 1:28 pm They fucking hold it. Duh.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sat Jul 29, 2006 2:02 pm I think they probably go before hand or just hold it in. The battles don't really last that long so I can't really see it being much of an issue.

[]:             Quote Post by Dimmi » Sat Jul 29, 2006 2:39 pm The plugsuits have built in nappies (sorry, couldn't resist :P)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 29, 2006 5:02 pm tinmeigut wrote: The LCL eventually became cloudy. I've been told that was some metabolic wastes from the pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 29, 2006 5:24 pm Maybe theiy wait till they get out, like tank pilots do.

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Sat Jul 29, 2006 6:11 pm Maybe it's part of the the life-support systems of the plug suit? 0_o

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 29, 2006 6:22 pm Umm your not really ment to be in those things longer then what 16 hours?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jul 29, 2006 10:48 pm Urine probably isn't such a big deal, as it tends to be sterile and 95% water to begin with. Solids, however, could be a very big deal.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Strangelove » Sun Jul 30, 2006 12:12 am Legend of the Past wrote: Dr. Strangelove wrote: Well, LCL is effectively, the same stuff you breethed for 9 months in your mom's womb. I'd imagine it really wouldn't make you need to..go..I supose. By that I mean, when you breath it in, it goes into your *lungs*, not your stomach.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jul 30, 2006 1:02 am BobBQ wrote: Urine probably isn't such a big deal, as it tends to be sterile and 95% water to begin with.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sun Jul 30, 2006 1:36 am There's probably a collection unit in each plug suits like with an Astronaut's space suit. Obviously though, they didn't adress that in the Anime since they didn't want people to think of the characters peeing whenever they're in a plugsuit.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 30, 2006 4:00 am BrikHaus81 wrote: BobBQ wrote: Urine probably isn't such a big deal, as it tends to be sterile and 95% water to begin with.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Jul 30, 2006 8:53 am Catheters. You realy don't want to know.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jul 30, 2006 9:11 am Legend of the Past wrote: BrikHaus81 wrote: BobBQ wrote: Urine probably isn't such a big deal, as it tends to be sterile and 95% water to begin with.
